headlines.title: Supreme Court allows abortion pill to remain available by mail nationwide
headlines.description: The Supreme Court ensured that the abortion pill, mifepristone, can continue to be available by mail without an in-person meeting with a clinician.

headlines.title: Americans from hantavirus cruise ship remain in quarantine to monitor for symptoms, CDC says
headlines.description: All 18 U.S. passengers who were on the MV Hondius cruise ship remain in federal quarantine as doctors and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention figure out when they can safely go home.

headlines.title: Florida surgeon who removed wrong organ says he is ‘forever traumatized’ by patient’s death
headlines.description: Thomas Shaknovsky said in a deposition in November that he believed he was removing 70-year-old William Bryan’s spleen, not his liver.

headlines.title: He pitched military service with a promise of lifetime healthcare. When he needed it, the system failed.
headlines.description: For more than a decade, Guy Shoemaker pitched military service to potential new recruits with one key promise: healthcare for life.“You’re going to have medical and dental for the rest of your life,” said Shoemaker, a retired Army sergeant first class and recruiter who spent a year in Afghanistan.

headlines.title: ‘A watershed moment’: A pancreatic cancer drug is set to transform treatment
headlines.description: An experimental pancreatic cancer drug that’s been shown to double survival in patients with advanced stages of the disease is poised to revolutionize the way the cancer is treated, oncologists say.

headlines.title: Dermatologist Shares the Must-Have Sunscreens for the Summer
headlines.description: Dr. Marnie Nussbaum, board-certified aesthetic dermatologist, joins TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones to share the must-have sunscreens that will help protect your skin this summer. They include Colorescience Brush On Shield, Sqween Sunpaint and more.

headlines.title: 3 Monitored for Hantavirus After Exposure to Cruise Passenger
headlines.description: State health officials in Kansas say three people with “a high-risk” exposure to an infected hantavirus cruise ship passenger have now been transferred to a Kansas City hospital “for observation.” Officials say they were not on the ship themselves and that the encounter happened abroad. Meanwhile, CDC staffers are in Omaha and interviewing the 16 quarantined Americans “about their exposure to confirmed cases.” NBC’s Maggie Vespa reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: CDC encourages cruise ship passengers to remain at Omaha facility
headlines.description: The CDC says it is encouraging the cruise ship passengers exposed to a deadly Hantavirus outbreak to remain at the special medical facility in Nebraska for the entirety of their 42 day monitoring period. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports.

headlines.title: Vance says Trump administration is withholding $1.3 billion in Medicaid payments to California
headlines.description: Vice president JD Vance announced that the Trump administration will be deferring $1.3 billion in Medicaid reimbursements to the state of California. Vance also said the administration will be sending letters to states to aggressively pursue Medicaid fraud or risk losing funds.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.
